skill.name: ai-audio-generation
skill.description: Generate AI audio, music, ambience, and sound effects through the hosted Image Skill runtime.
skill.summary: Create hosted AI audio from prompts with guided cost checks and recoverable jobs.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: Static command execution findings are confirmed as intentional documentation for remote npm commands, not embedded executable code. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives on prose lines, while network and filesystem findings reflect hosted service URLs and hidden agent state paths. Publish with a warning because runtime use depends on remote packages, hosted APIs, and possible media spend.

medium_findings.title: Remote Package Execution Through npx
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run remote npm packages with npx, including image-skill and skills.sh commands. This is a real supply-chain and command execution risk, although the commands match the skill purpose and no malicious payload is present in the repository.
medium_findings.recommendation: Warn users that runtime commands fetch external packages. Prefer pinned versions and review package provenance before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced commands clearly execute remote npm packages at runtime. The surrounding text is consistent with legitimate hosted audio generation, so malicious intent is not confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Hidden Agent State Path Modification
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends changing HOME and CODEX_HOME to workspace-local hidden directories before installing the skill. This can alter agent profile state and create hidden files, but it is presented as a workaround for read-only global targets.
medium_findings.recommendation: Tell users before changing agent home paths and avoid reusing hidden state directories across unrelated workspaces.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands explicitly export HOME and CODEX_HOME and create hidden directories. The stated purpose is operational, but the behavior can affect agent state.

low_findings.title: Expected Hosted Service URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the skill homepage, documentation, canonical contract, and hosted API. Network access is expected for a hosted audio generation skill, with no evidence of credential exfiltration.
low_findings.recommendation: Document that the skill depends on image-skill.com and api.image-skill.com for hosted audio generation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible service endpoints that match the advertised hosted runtime. No suspicious destination or hidden data transfer is present in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: Static weak cryptography alerts on the description, token description, and usage text are false positives. No cryptographic function, hashing call, cipher selection, or encoded crypto material appears on those lines.
low_findings.recommendation: Dismiss these weak cryptography alerts unless new code is added that performs cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows only YAML prose and usage text at the reported lines. There is no crypto implementation or weak algorithm choice to evaluate.

dangerous_patterns.title: Runtime Remote Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflow relies on npx commands that fetch and execute external packages during agent operation.
dangerous_patterns.recommendation: Use pinned package versions and require user approval before running spend-capable generation commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command blocks directly show npx execution. The risk is supply-chain and spend related, not confirmed malware.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workspace Hidden State Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The fallback install path uses hidden workspace directories for agent and Codex state.
dangerous_patterns.recommendation: Keep hidden state isolated per workspace and disclose profile path changes before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands explicitly create and use hidden directories. The text gives a legitimate reason, but state redirection remains sensitive.

content.user_title: Generate Hosted AI Audio with Agent Commands
content.value_statement: Agents often need audio output without managing provider accounts or local model servers. This skill routes prompts through Image Skill for guided hosted generation, cost checks, and durable media links.
actual_capabilities: Starts a no-spend guide for audio generation tasks.
Runs hosted text-to-audio generation through Image Skill commands.
Keeps hosted media URLs, recoverable jobs, and cost receipts in one workflow.
Provides installation commands for the intent skill and canonical Image Skill entry.
Documents feedback reporting when a needed audio capability is missing.
limitations: Requires network access to Image Skill hosted services.
Uses remote npm commands that should be reviewed before execution.
May require media spend approval before creating audio.
Does not run local audio models or provide provider-specific credentials.

use_cases.title: Prototype Product Sounds
use_cases.description: Generate short interface sounds, alerts, or ambience for product prototypes with hosted links.

use_cases.title: Create Audio Drafts
use_cases.description: Turn a creative brief into music or sound design drafts without setting up provider accounts.

use_cases.title: Automate Agent Media Tasks
use_cases.description: Use repeatable commands for audio jobs, receipts, and recoverable outputs in agent workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Sound
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one hosted audio asset.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AI audio generation skill to create a warm synth bed. Start with the no-spend guide and ask before spending.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Sound Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three prompt directions for a calm app notification sound, then run the safest guided Image Skill workflow for the best option.

prompt_templates.title: Design Audio for a Scene
prompt_templates.scenario: Produce ambience for a specific environment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate hosted ambience for a quiet science lab at night. Include cost guidance, expected output links, and recovery details.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Controlled Audio Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with cost and provenance controls.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan and execute an Image Skill audio generation run with a spend cap, model selection, command review, receipt capture, and feedback if the model fails.

output_examples.input: Create a soft looping ambience for a meditation app.
output_examples.output: The assistant starts with the guide, confirms spend permission, creates the hosted audio, and returns a durable media link with receipt details.

output_examples.input: Make a short confirmation sound for a mobile checkout flow.
output: A concise prompt is prepared for a friendly, non-intrusive confirmation tone.
The assistant runs the guided command path and records the hosted output location.
The result includes recovery notes for future agents.

best_practices: Run the no-spend guide before creating media.
Review remote npx commands and package provenance before execution.
Set a clear spend cap and keep receipt details with each output.
anti_patterns: Do not provide third-party provider API keys to this skill.
Do not run generation commands when media spend is not approved.
Do not reuse hidden agent state directories across unrelated projects.

faq.question: Does this skill generate audio locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the hosted Image Skill runtime and documented CLI commands.

faq.question: Does it require provider API keys?
faq.answer: No. The skill says not to bring provider keys or create provider accounts.

faq.question: Can it create music and sound effects?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill targets music, ambience, effects, and general text-to-audio tasks.

faq.question: Is spending required?
faq.answer: Not for the guide. Creating media may require explicit spend approval and available credits.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The workflow runs remote npm commands and uses hosted services, so users should review commands before execution.

faq.question: What happens if a needed model is missing?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the agent to use a fallback for that gap and submit hosted feedback.
